title: All-CLI-families quota exhaustion must checkpoint-and-defer, not fail-forward (cycle-393's fix half-implemented; 3rd occurrence of the quota-stall class)
summary: When EVERY CLI family in a source-writing phase's fallback chain returns exit=85 (quota), the loop keeps spending attempts against a resource that resets in hours: cycle 656 burned 4 build attempts (claude->codex->claude->codex, all 85), a 300s artifact-timeout, a no-effect nudge, ~10.5 min wall-clock, then a FAIL plus a quota-consuming retrospective - destroying nothing but its own quota, while completed scout/triage/premise/TDD work and a correct RED contract sat ready for a clean resume. Cycle-393's cross-family failover is live and worked (build DID alternate families); it left the ALL-families-exhausted terminal case unhandled. Signature: llm-calls.ndjson consecutive exit=85 across every family in the chain.
root_cause: Wrong assumption: 'a source-writing phase can always make forward progress by retrying' - quota pressure is treated as per-attempt transient. There is no fleet-wide terminal-exhaustion detector, so the failover chain with zero remaining targets degenerates into burn-to-FAIL. The checkpoint machinery ALREADY EXISTS (quota-boundary checkpoints with resumeFromPhase + quotaResetAt fields are written at loop boundaries; evolve loop --resume consumes them) - it is just never triggered mid-cycle by all-families exhaustion.
fix: Deterministic detector at the phase-dispatch seam: when consecutive attempts for a source-writing phase return exit=85 across EVERY family in the routing profile's fallback chain (signalcenter LivenessExhausted + cli-health benches are existing inputs), STOP spending attempts; write a quota-boundary checkpoint (resumeFromPhase=<this phase>, reason=all-families-exhausted, quotaResetAt from usageprobe/ParseResetHint when available) preserving completed phases + the worktree RED contract; end the cycle as DEFERRED (a distinct C1 outcome, not FAIL - no retro burn against drained quota); the loop then either proceeds to next batch work that needs no LLM or exits cleanly for evolve loop --resume. Config-driven threshold via policy (compiled default: chain exhausted once = defer). Do NOT re-classify genuine single-family exhaustion (existing bench+failover unchanged).
acceptance: Regression test reproducing cycle-656: fake bridge returning exit=85 for every family => phase stops after one full chain pass, quota-boundary checkpoint written with resumeFromPhase and completed phases preserved, cycle outcome DEFERRED (not FAIL), no retro dispatched
Single-family 85 with a healthy sibling family still fails over normally (no behavior change - table-driven)
evolve loop --resume from the written checkpoint re-enters at the deferred phase and completes when the fake provider recovers
C1 chokepoint records the DEFERRED outcome with abort_reason=all-families-exhausted (no FAILED_UNEXPLAINED)
go test -race on touched packages PASS; apicover clean
source: operator-injection from cycle-656 retrospective preventive actions (2026-07-10)
